channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump and Xi Begin Key Bilateral Talks in Beijing Amid Calls for Cooperation
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has commenced a significant summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, signalling potential shifts in US-China relations. The meeting began with a formal handshak...

item.title: Minister Malatsi Highlights Need for Meaningful Digital Access in South Africa
item.description: Communications and Digital Technologies Minister Solly Malatsi emphasised in Parliament that while South Africa has made significant progress in connecting its citizens to the internet, simply providi...

item.title: Iran Hosts Ceremony to Send National Football Team to 2026 World Cup
item.description: Iran don hold big send-off ceremony for their national football team as dem dey prepare for 2026 World Cup wey go take place for United States, Mexico, and Canada. People gather for central Tehran squ...

item.title: Mother sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering her husband with fentanyl
item.description: Kouri Richins, a 36-year-old mother from the United States, has been sentenced to life in prison without parole after being found guilty of murdering her husband, Eric Richins, in 2022. The case gaine...

item.title: Cardoso confident Sundowns can bounce back for CAF Champions League final after Premiership loss
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns endured a disappointing finish to their domestic campaign after a 3-2 defeat to TS Galaxy, which handed Orlando Pirates a potential edge to claim the Betway Premiership title. Despit...

item.title: Tanzania vehicle imports surge as demand for fuel-efficient cars rises
item.description: In the first quarter of 2026, Tanzania saw a big jump in spending on imported vehicles, reaching Sh352.84 billion, up 53.9% from the same period in 2025. Consumers are now preferring small, fuel-effic...

item.title: Key Developments in Nebraska Democratic Primaries Highlight Strategic Moves
item.description: In the recent Nebraska Democratic primaries, unexpected developments reshaped the political landscape. Denise Powell has emerged as the projected winner of the Democratic nomination for a pivotal Hous...

item.title: Mafia Island faces severe transport disruption due to ferry repairs
item.description: Residents of Mafia District in Coast Region are facing serious transport problems after the MV Kilindoni ferry broke down on May 12, 2026. Since then, locals have had to depend on dhows and small boat...

item.title: US and China Leaders Hold Strategic Talks in Beijing on Trade, Iran and Taiwan
item.description: US President Donald Trump visited Beijing to hold a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, addressing critical issues affecting global politics and economy. The leaders were welcomed with f...

item.title: Senator Bato Dela Rosa Exits Senate Premises Early Thursday
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa reportedly left the Senate premises early Thursday morning, according to fellow senators. While details surrounding his early departure were not disclosed, sources indi...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa Leaves Senate Premises Early Thursday
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa has reportedly left the Senate, according to confirmation from another senator. Based on information from a member of the Senate secretariat, Dela Rosa departed the Sen...

item.title: ConCourt ruling reinforces the obligation of Parliament to investigate presidential misconduct
item.description: The Constitutional Court of South Africa recently delivered a landmark judgment in EFF and Another v Speaker of the National Assembly and Others, which has major implications for the country's democra...

item.title: Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds Begin Studio Sessions with Potential New Album in Mind
item.description: Nick Cave has disclosed that The Bad Seeds are currently spending time in the studio, though their work is informal and exploratory, motivated only by the vague possibility of producing a new album. T...

item.title: Preview and Tips for Scone Race Day
item.description: The Canberra Times provides a comprehensive guide for racegoers heading to Scone on Saturday, highlighting key contenders across ten races. In Race 1, Viewpoint is tipped to rebound after a disappoint...

item.title: Judge Dismisses Alberta Separatist Petition, Upholds First Nations Rights
item.description: A judge has overturned a petition advocating for Alberta's separation from Canada, ruling in favour of First Nations groups. The decision has elicited strong reactions from various political figures a...

item.title: Developers Report Skill Erosion and Frustration with AI-Assisted Coding
item.description: A growing number of software developers are expressing concern over their use of AI for coding, reporting that it often creates more work rather than saving time. According to a report by 404 Media, d...

item.title: Sanral to manage additional provincial roads amid funding and maintenance challenges
item.description: Since 2013, provincial governments have handed over more than 13,000km of roads to the South African National Roads Agency (Sanral) for management and maintenance. Transport Minister Barbara Creecy hi...

item.title: Elizabeth Montgomery’s Iconic Role as Samantha Stephens in “Bewitched”: A Photo Retrospective
item.description: Elizabeth Montgomery became one of television's most memorable leading ladies through her role as Samantha Stephens on the ABC sitcom 'Bewitched,' which aired from 1964 to 1972. Over the course of all...

item.title: Challenges and Strategies for Achieving Sustainable AI
item.description: AI sustainability is facing significant challenges as tech giants expand data centers powered largely by fossil fuels, despite earlier commitments to reduce emissions. Researcher Sasha Luccioni, a spe...

item.title: Students from Waterloo Region Participate in Cultural Exchange with Northern Quebec Inuit Community
item.description: A group of students from the Waterloo Region recently embarked on a cultural exchange program with a remote Inuit community in northern Quebec. The initiative aimed to foster understanding, respect, a...
